evals.prompt: I want to build a Stream Deck plugin that controls OBS Studio. I need buttons for: start/stop recording, toggle streaming, mute/unmute microphone, and a scene switcher. The scene switcher should show the current scene name on the button. I have a Stream Deck MK.2 (15 keys). OBS has a WebSocket API I can connect to. Build me the full plugin.
evals.expected_output: A complete TypeScript plugin project with: manifest.json with all 4 actions, action files for record/stream/mute/scene, OBS WebSocket connection handling, dynamic scene name on button using setTitle, rollup build config, and install instructions. Should NOT use PKCE (WebSocket doesn't need it) — just connect to local OBS WebSocket.
assertions: manifest.json exists with UUID in reverse-DNS format
manifest.json contains all 4 action UUIDs
manifest.json has CodePath pointing to bin/plugin.js
plugin.ts exists and calls streamDeck.connect()
All 4 action TypeScript files exist
Scene action uses setTitle() to show current scene name
rollup.config.mjs exists
package.json includes @elgato/streamdeck dependency
No PKCE OAuth code (not needed for local WebSocket)
Tokens/secrets stored in global settings if any auth is needed

evals.prompt: I have a Stream Deck Plus and want a plugin that shows my computer's CPU usage, RAM usage, and network activity on the first 3 encoder dials. Rotating a dial should do nothing (read-only display), but pressing any dial should refresh all stats immediately. The touch strip should show a bar graph of the current value. Build the full plugin.
evals.expected_output: Plugin with 3 encoder actions (cpu, ram, network), each using setFeedback with $A1 layout showing current value and indicator bar. onDialDown triggers a stat refresh. Polling loop updates every 2-3 seconds using Node's os module. Manifest has all 3 actions with Controllers: [Encoder]. No OAuth needed.
assertions: manifest.json has 3 encoder actions with Controllers: [Encoder]
Each action uses Encoder.layout set to a built-in layout like $A1
Each action calls action.setFeedback() with indicator field
action.isDial() guard present before setFeedback calls
Node os module used to get system stats (os.cpus, os.freemem, etc.)
onDialDown triggers stat refresh
Polling interval set up in plugin.ts
plugin.ts calls await streamDeck.connect() at the end
package.json and rollup.config.mjs present

evals.prompt: I want a Stream Deck plugin that connects to the GitHub API. When I press a button it should show how many open PRs I have assigned to me, and pressing it opens the GitHub PR page in my browser. I need to authenticate with GitHub using OAuth. I'm on a Stream Deck MK.2. Build it.
evals.expected_output: Complete plugin with PKCE OAuth flow for GitHub (or PAT-based simpler auth), a button action that fetches PRs via GitHub API, shows count as button title, opens github.com/pulls on press. Should use global settings for token storage. Should include a property inspector for entering GitHub credentials/starting auth.
assertions: manifest.json present with valid UUID and action
Auth uses either PKCE flow OR personal access token stored in global settings
Token stored in streamDeck.settings.setGlobalSettings (NOT action settings)
GitHub API called with Authorization header
Button title updated with PR count using setTitle()
streamDeck.system.openUrl() used to open browser
Property inspector HTML file exists
plugin.ts registers action and calls streamDeck.connect()
rollup.config.mjs and package.json present
